title: [v3.1-dev] [rootless] detection of id > 0 doesn't work for storage
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
When running podman as a non-root user (id > 0|1000), local _storage.conf_ gets ignored and an error is thrown. This also happens, while all needed parameters are giving in command line.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
target="/home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin"
alias podman="PATH=$PATH:$target $target/podman \
--conmon $target/conmon \
--network-cmd-path $target/slirp4netns \
--root=$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage \
--runtime $target/crun \
--storage-driver overlay \
--storage-opt 'overlay.mount_program=$target/fuse-overlayfs'"

podman run -it --rm bash
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
ERRO[0000] The storage 'driver' option must be set in /etc/containers/storage.conf, guarantee proper operation.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Don't show this error, since all needed parameters are provided in the command line and in the user's storage.conf.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Container gets executed as expected, podman doesn't fail on the error.
This is a regression. It has appeared in pre-v1, v1, and v2 :-)

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.0-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.14
Git Commit:   3ba0afdd0f03195827f46b50757928ead44b1567
Built:        Sat Feb 13 19:38:35 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
@mheon @rhatdan no need to.

There was an old global _storage.conf_ in the system! Since this gets read first, the error appears. Maybe a warning would be better?
